source_name: Zillow: Iowa City, IA Housing Market — ZHVI & ZORI
section: Zillow Home Value Index (ZHVI) — Citywide Overview
text:
As of February 28, 2026, the Zillow Home Value Index (ZHVI) for Iowa City, IA is $292,103,
representing a 4.6 percent increase over the prior year. The ZHVI is built from monthly
changes in property-level Zestimates and is designed to capture both the level and
directional change of home values across geographies and housing types. Current market
metrics for Iowa City (data through February 28, 2026 unless noted):

 Typical Home Value (ZHVI): $292,103 (+4.6% year-over-year)
 Median Sale Price: $289,417 (as of January 31, 2026)
 Median List Price: $297,567
 Median Sale-to-List Ratio: 0.983 (98.3%)
 For-Sale Inventory: 248 active listings
 New Listings: 54 (February 28, 2026)
 Percent of Sales Over List: 10.2%
 Percent of Sales Under List: 68.1%
 Median Days to Pending: 56 days

The 0.983 sale-to-list ratio indicates buyers are on average negotiating approximately
1.7 percent off asking price — a modestly buyer-favorable dynamic compared to the
competitive conditions of 2021–2022.

source_name: Zillow: Iowa City, IA Housing Market — ZHVI & ZORI
text:
Zillow Home Value Index (ZHVI) by Iowa City neighborhood (data through February 2026):

 South Pointe: $314,570 (highest among tracked neighborhoods)
 Pepperwood: $283,676
 Oak Grove: $242,293
 Wetherby: $241,441
 Creekside: $232,169
 Lucas Farms: $223,431
 Grant Wood: $218,543
 Broadway: $96,298 (lowest — likely reflects high share of multi-family/student housing)
 Paddock: n/a (insufficient data)

South Pointe commands a roughly 44 percent premium over Broadway, reflecting the wide
variation in housing type, tenure, and proximity to the University of Iowa across Iowa
City's neighborhoods. Grant Wood, Wetherby, and Creekside cluster in the $218,000–$242,000
range, suggesting relative affordability in the city's south and east sides.

source_name: Zillow: Iowa City, IA Housing Market — ZHVI & ZORI
section: Zillow Observed Rent Index (ZORI) — Iowa City
text:
Iowa City Rental Market (Zillow Observed Rent Index / ZORI, as of February 28, 2026):

 Average Rent (Iowa City): $1,308/month
 National Average Rent: $1,895/month
 Month-Over-Month Change: +0.4%
 Year-Over-Year Change: +4.3%

Iowa City average rents are approximately 31 percent below the national average, reflecting
both Iowa's generally lower cost of living and the dominance of student rental housing —
which compresses market-rate rents by increasing supply near the University of Iowa campus.
The 4.3 percent year-over-year rent increase outpaces general inflation, suggesting continued
demand pressure in the rental market despite the availability of affordable for-sale options.
The Zillow Observed Rent Index controls for changes in the quality of available rental
inventory, making it a cleaner measure of rent-level trends than raw asking-price averages.
